COL.text:
## Reading
Colombia are in the Round of 16 and arrive with momentum. They won Group K without losing and, in the Round of 32, beat Ghana with an early goal and plenty of defensive solidity, confirming their strong form.
## Performance
In the group they earned seven points: 3-1 over Uzbekistan, 1-0 over DR Congo and a 0-0 with Portugal that secured first place. In the Round of 32, Jhon Arias settled it early in the 14th minute and Colombia held the 1-0 to the end, with no major scares against Ghana.
## By the numbers
Four matches, three wins and one draw; 5 goals scored and only 1 conceded — the defense is the great strength. Winners of Group K, Colombia face Switzerland in the Round of 16 on July 7.

POR.text:
## Reading
Roberto Martínez's Portugal advanced to the Round of 16 the hard way: they came from behind to beat Croatia 2-1 in the Round of 32, with Cristiano Ronaldo equalizing from the penalty spot and Gonçalo Ramos deciding it in stoppage time. The captain scored his first goal in a World Cup knockout match and keeps the Portuguese dream alive.
## Performance
They qualified second in Group K. They drew with DR Congo (1-1), thrashed Uzbekistan 5-0 (two for Ronaldo) and were held to a 0-0 by Colombia, surrendering first place. A solid defense (only one goal conceded), but an inconsistent attack against the more qualified sides.
## Round of 32
Croatia went ahead through Ivan Perisic (53'). Ronaldo equalized from the spot (68') — his first goal in a World Cup knockout match — and, with the game heading toward extra time, Gonçalo Ramos appeared at 90+4' to complete the 2-1 comeback.
## By the numbers
GP4 · 2-1 comeback win over Croatia in the Round of 32 · 2nd in Group K (5 points, GD +5 in the Group Stage). Cristiano Ronaldo finally broke through in the knockouts. Qualified for the Round of 16, where they face Spain.

COD.text:
## Reading
Sébastien Desabre's DR Congo made history by reaching the knockouts as one of the best third-placed teams, and even gave England a scare: they went ahead in the Round of 32 but were turned around and lost 2-1, ending their run with their heads held high.
## Performance
They finished 3rd in Group K with 4 points, qualifying among the best third-placed teams. They drew with Portugal (1-1), lost to Colombia (1-0) and beat Uzbekistan 3-1 in the decisive round (two for Yoane Wissa, top scorer with three goals).
## Round of 32
Against England, Brian Cipenga silenced the favorites early at 7'. The Leopards held the lead until halftime, but Harry Kane appeared twice in the second half (75' and 86') to turn the game around. Noah Sadiki was booked in the 27th minute.
## By the numbers
GP4 · 2-1 loss to England in the Round of 32 · eliminated. They had qualified as one of the eight best third-placed teams (3rd in Group K, 4 points) — an unprecedented feat. Top scorer: Yoane Wissa. The end of a historic campaign.

UZB.text:
## Reading
Historic World Cup debutants, Fabio Cannavaro's Uzbekistan bowed out in the Group Stage, without a point and with a defense in tatters: 11 goals conceded, by far the worst record in Group K. In their first appearance, the team felt the gap in level to the favorites and finish bottom, eliminated.
## Performance
They debuted losing to Colombia 3-1, with Abbosbek Fayzullaev scoring the Uzbek goal. Next, they were overrun by Portugal 5-0, with no answer to the Portuguese attack. In the finale, they lived their cruelest chapter: they opened the scoring against DR Congo through Eldor Shomurodov early in the 10th minute, but conceded the comeback and lost 3-1, letting slip the chance for a positive send-off.
## By the numbers
GP3 · 3 losses · 2 goals scored · 11 conceded (GD -9) · 4th and last in Group K, eliminated. Goals from Abbosbek Fayzullaev and Eldor Shomurodov (one each). End of the road: Uzbekistan bid farewell to their first World Cup with hard lessons at the back.

ENG.text:
## Reading
Thomas Tuchel's England had to sweat, but advanced: they fell behind and came from behind to beat DR Congo 2-1 in the Round of 32, with two goals from Harry Kane in the second half. Unbeaten since the start of the tournament, they march on firmly into the knockouts.
## Performance
They finished top of Group L and unbeaten: 4-2 over Croatia in the opener (two for Kane), 0-0 with Ghana and 2-0 over Panama. Firepower with Kane and squad depth — Saka, Eze and Rashford on the bench — were the trump cards.
## Round of 32
DR Congo silenced the English through Brian Cipenga early at 7'. But England piled on the pressure and Harry Kane settled it down the stretch, with a brace (75' and 86') that completed the comeback. Jude Bellingham was booked in the 19th minute.
## By the numbers
GP4 · 2-1 comeback win over DR Congo in the Round of 32 · unbeaten winners of Group L (7 points, GD +4 in the Group Stage). Top scorer: Harry Kane, decisive once again. Coach: Thomas Tuchel. Qualified for the Round of 16, where they face Mexico.

GHA.text:
## Reading
Ghana's run ended in the Round of 32. Qualified as one of the best third-placed teams from Group L, they ran into a solid Colombia and lost 1-0, lacking the finishing touch to dream of the Round of 16.
## Performance
In the group, Ghana did enough: 1-0 over Panama, 0-0 with England and a 2-1 loss to Croatia — four points and a spot via the third-place route. In the Round of 32, they conceded Jhon Arias's goal early at 14 and, despite pressing in the second half, could not find the equalizer against the Colombian rearguard.
## By the numbers
Four matches, one win, one draw and two losses; only 2 goals scored and 3 conceded — the unproductive attack weighed heavily. Third in Group L, Ghana are eliminated in the Round of 32.

PAN.text:
## Reading
Panama bid farewell to the World Cup without a point and without scoring a goal. The three losses in Group L, all by a narrow margin, left the team bottom of the group.
## Performance
They lost to Ghana 1-0, to Croatia 1-0 and to England 2-0, never once finding the opposition net.
## By the numbers
GP3 · 3 losses · 0 goals scored · 4 conceded (GD -4) · bottom of Group L with 0 points. Eliminated in the Group Stage.

CRO.text:
## Reading
Croatia bid farewell to the World Cup in the Round of 32: they went ahead against Portugal, but conceded the comeback and lost 2-1. Likely the last World Cup for Luka Modrić, who left the field after a dignified campaign.
## Performance
They qualified 2nd in Group L. They lost to England 4-2 in the opener, but reacted with hard-fought wins over Panama (1-0) and Ghana (2-1), with Modrić orchestrating the midfield.
## Round of 32
Against Portugal, Ivan Perisic opened the scoring (53') and left the Croatians close to the Round of 16. But Cristiano Ronaldo equalized from the spot (68') and Gonçalo Ramos settled it in stoppage time (90+4'), sealing the 2-1 elimination. Modrić was booked in the 59th minute.
## By the numbers
GP4 · 2-1 loss to Portugal in the Round of 32 · eliminated. They had qualified 2nd in Group L (6 points). End of the road at the World Cup for Luka Modrić's generation.

FRA.text:
## Reading
Didier Deschamps's France are in the Quarterfinals and remain at 100%. After thrashing Sweden in the Round of 32, they needed patience to overcome a very compact Paraguay — and, as almost always, Kylian Mbappé appeared to settle it. Les Bleus reach the business end of the knockouts as one of the major title contenders.
## Performance
They closed out Group I with three wins and the best attack in the section: 3-1 over Senegal, 3-0 over Iraq and 4-1 over Norway, with a show from Ousmane Dembélé (three goals). In the Round of 32, they overran Sweden 3-0 (Mbappé twice, at 45' and 74', and Barcola at 53'). In the Round of 16, against a battling Paraguay, they broke the resistance only at 70', through Mbappé, in the 1-0 that earned the spot.
## By the numbers
Five matches, five wins — a perfect record. Unbeaten winners of Group I with 9 points (GD +8 in the first round). In the Round of 32, 3-0 over Sweden; in the Round of 16, 1-0 over Paraguay. Top scorer: Kylian Mbappé (7 goals). Next challenge: Morocco, in the Quarterfinals.

NOR.text:
## Reading
Erling Haaland's Norway stayed alive in the World Cup: they beat Ivory Coast 2-1 in the Round of 32 and advanced to the Round of 16. As always, the number 9 appeared at the decisive moment to stamp the ticket.
## Performance
They qualified second in Group I. They lost to France 4-1 in the opener, but reacted with two wins — 3-2 over Senegal (two for Haaland) and 4-1 over Iraq (two more from the top scorer). Haaland closed the Group Stage with four goals.
## Round of 32
Against the Elephants, Norway went ahead through Antonio Nusa (39'), saw Amad Diallo equalize (74') and had to sweat until Erling Haaland decided it at 86', securing the 2-1 and the spot.
## By the numbers
GP4 · 2-1 win over Ivory Coast in the Round of 32 · runners-up in Group I (6 points, GD +1 in the Group Stage). Top scorer: Erling Haaland. Qualified for the Round of 16, where they face Brazil.

SEN.text:
## Reading
Senegal were a step away from advancing, but saw the dream collapse: they went up 2-0 over Belgium in the Round of 32, conceded the equalizer late and were eliminated 3-2 in extra time. A bitter goodbye after dominating much of the game.
## Performance
They had qualified as one of the best third-placed teams in Group I, with 3 points. They lost to France (3-1) and Norway (3-2, two for Sarr), but thrashed Iraq 5-0 in the final round to secure the spot on goal difference.
## Round of 32
Against Belgium, Senegal played the perfect game for more than 80 minutes: Habib Diarra (24') and Ismaila Sarr (51') made it 2-0. But Romelu Lukaku (86') and Youri Tielemans (89') equalized, and the same Tielemans decided it in extra time (120+5'), eliminating the Lions of Teranga 3-2.
## By the numbers
GP4 · 3-2 extra-time loss to Belgium in the Round of 32 · eliminated. They had qualified among the eight best third-placed teams (3rd in Group I, 3 points). Top scorer: Ismaila Sarr. End of the campaign.

IRQ.text:
## Reading
Iraq had a campaign to forget in Group I: three losses, no points and a defense that collapsed, conceding 12 goals in three matches. The team bids farewell to the World Cup at the bottom of the group.
## Performance
They lost to France 3-0, conceded 4-1 to Norway — Aymen Hussein scored, but also put one in his own net in stoppage time — and were thrashed by Senegal 5-0 in the final round.
## By the numbers
GP3 · 3 losses · 1 goal scored · 12 conceded (GD -11) · bottom of Group I with 0 points. Eliminated in the Group Stage.

NED.text:
## Reading
Ronald Koeman's Netherlands dominated Group F, but bid farewell to the World Cup right away in the Round of 32. Clear leaders of the section with 7 points and the most productive attack in the group (10 goals), the Oranje reached the knockouts unbeaten — and fell on penalties to Morocco, without losing in regulation.
## Performance
In the Group Stage, they drew 2-2 with Japan (Van Dijk and Summerville), overran Sweden 5-1 (two for Brobbey, two for Gakpo) and beat Tunisia 3-1 — an unbeaten campaign that secured first place. In the Round of 32, the script turned to drama: Gakpo opened the scoring in the 72nd minute, but Issa Diop equalized at 90+1 and took the game to extra time. Goalless in the extra period, the decision went to a penalty shootout, and Morocco won 3-2 — Koopmeiners and Weghorst converted, but Kluivert, Timber and Summerville missed.
## By the numbers
Group Stage: GP3 · 2 wins · 1 draw · 10 goals scored · 4 conceded (GD +6) · 1st in Group F with 7 points. Top scorer: Brian Brobbey (3 goals). In the Round of 32, a 1-1 draw with Morocco and elimination on penalties (2-3). End of the road for the Netherlands at the World Cup.

JPN.text:
## Reading
Hajime Moriyasu's Japan bid farewell to the World Cup in the Round of 32. Runners-up in Group F, they went toe to toe with Brazil and even went ahead, but conceded the comeback in the second half and were eliminated 2-1. They end the World Cup unbeaten in the Group Stage and with the feeling that they were close to a historic upset.
## Performance
In the first round they drew 2-2 with the Netherlands, thrashed Tunisia 4-0 and were held to a 1-1 by Sweden. In the Round of 32, Kaishu Sano opened the scoring against Brazil (29'), but Casemiro (56') and Gabriel Martinelli (90+5') turned it around for the Seleção. An intense and organized side, they lacked the ability to hold the result against a giant.
## By the numbers
Group Stage: 1W 2D, 7 goals scored, 3 conceded (GD +4), 2nd in Group F (5 pts). In the Round of 32, eliminated by Brazil 2-1. Standout in the first round: top scorer Ayase Ueda.

SWE.text:
## Reading
Graham Potter's Sweden reached the Round of 32 as one of the best third-placed teams, but ran into France: they lost 3-0 and bid farewell to the World Cup. The attack of Isak and Gyökeres had no answer to French power.
## Performance
They finished 3rd in Group F with 4 points: they thrashed Tunisia 5-1 in the opener, but conceded 5-1 to the Netherlands and drew 1-1 with Japan. Defensive wobbles marked the campaign, salvaged by the goal difference from the opening rout.
## Round of 32
Against France, there was no story: Kylian Mbappé (45'), Bradley Barcola (53') and Mbappé again (74') sealed the 3-0 that eliminated the Swedes.
## By the numbers
GP4 · 3-0 loss to France in the Round of 32 · eliminated. They had qualified as one of the eight best third-placed teams (3rd in Group F, 4 points). End of the campaign.

TUN.text:
## Reading
Hervé Renard's Tunisia had a campaign to forget and are eliminated: they lost all three matches in Group F and finished bottom, with the worst goal difference of the Group Stage. The defense, fragile in every game, was the big problem.
## Performance
They conceded 5-1 to Sweden in the opener (goal from Omar Rekik), were thrashed 4-0 by Japan and lost 3-1 to the Netherlands, pulling one back through Hazem Mastouri. They conceded 12 goals in three matches and never managed to balance the contests.
## By the numbers
GP3 · 3 losses · 2 goals scored · 12 conceded (GD -10) · bottom of Group F, without points. Eliminated in the Group Stage.

CAN.text:
## Reading
Jesse Marsch's Canada's home World Cup ended in the Round of 16. After a historic campaign — the best by a host nation at a World Cup — the Canadians ran into a far superior Morocco and fell 3-0. The positive balance remains: getting past the Group Stage and winning a knockout match for the first time.
## Performance
In the first round, they drew with Bosnia (1-1), thrashed Qatar 6-0 — a hat-trick from Jonathan David — and lost to Switzerland 2-1. In the Round of 32, they got past South Africa 1-0 with a goal from Stephen Eustáquio in stoppage time (90+2'). In the Round of 16, however, they had no answer for Morocco: Azzedine Ounahi scored twice (50' and 82') and Soufiane Rahimi completed the 3-0 in stoppage time. David, the team's top scorer, was even booked at 43 and couldn't find the net.
## By the numbers
Five matches: 2 wins, 1 draw and 2 losses. In the Group Stage, 2nd in Group B (4 pts, 8 goals scored and 3 conceded, GD +5). In the Round of 32, 1-0 over South Africa; in the Round of 16, 0-3 to Morocco — eliminated. Top scorer: Jonathan David (3 goals).

SUI.text:
## Reading
Switzerland are in the Round of 16 and live up to the status they carried. They won Group B with authority and, in the Round of 32, dispatched Algeria without any scares, keeping the efficient edge that has taken them so far.
## Performance
In the Group Stage they earned seven points: a 1-1 draw with Qatar in the opener, followed by 4-1 over Bosnia and 2-1 over Canada — the best campaign in the group. In the Round of 32, they settled it early against Algeria: Breel Embolo opened the scoring in the 10th minute and Dan Ndoye doubled it early at 46, in the 2-0 that stamped the ticket.
## By the numbers
Four matches, three wins and one draw, 9 goals scored and only 4 conceded. Winners of Group B, Switzerland now face Colombia in the Round of 16 on July 7.

QAT.text:
## Reading
Julen Lopetegui's Qatar had a Group Stage to forget and finished bottom of Group B, eliminated. They earned just 1 point — the draw with Switzerland in the opener — and lost the following two matches, conceding 10 goals in total. The campaign exposed the limitations and indiscipline of a team that disappointed.
## Performance
After the promising 1-1 with Switzerland, Qatar collapsed: they conceded 6-0 to Canada, with two sendings-off, and lost 3-1 to Bosnia in the send-off, pulling one back through Hassan Al-Haydos (42). A fragile defense and a lack of consistency marked the worst campaign in the group.
## By the numbers
GP3 · 1 draw · 2 losses · 2 goals scored · 10 conceded (GD -8) · bottom of Group B with 1 point. Eliminated in the Group Stage.

BIH.text:
## Reading
Sergej Barbarez's Bosnia and Herzegovina advanced to the knockouts among the best third-placed teams, but fell against the host nation: they lost to the United States 2-0 and bowed out of the World Cup in the Round of 32.
## Performance
They finished 3rd in Group B with 4 points: a draw with Canada (1-1), a 4-1 loss to Switzerland and a 3-1 win over Qatar in the send-off. The heavy defeat against the Swiss cost them the goal difference in the fight for the 2nd direct spot.
## Round of 32
Against the United States, Bosnia stalled against the opposition attack: Folarin Balogun opened the scoring (45') and, even with the forward sent off in the 64th minute, Malik Tillman doubled it (82') to seal the 2-0.
## By the numbers
GP4 · 2-0 loss to the United States in the Round of 32 · eliminated. They had qualified among the eight best third-placed teams (3rd in Group B, 4 points). End of the campaign.

USA.text:
## Reading
Mauricio Pochettino's United States, the host nation, keep dreaming: they beat Bosnia and Herzegovina 2-0 in the Round of 32 and advanced to the Round of 16, even while playing almost the entire second half a man down after Balogun's sending-off.
## Performance
They advanced as winners of Group D with 6 points: 4-1 over Paraguay (two for Balogun), 2-0 over Australia and a 3-2 loss to Turkey when already qualified. A productive attack, with Folarin Balogun standing out.
## Round of 32
Against Bosnia, Balogun opened the scoring at the end of the first half (45'), but was sent off in the 64th minute, leaving the U.S. with ten. Even so, Malik Tillman doubled it (82') and secured the 2-0 and qualification.
## By the numbers
GP4 · 2-0 win over Bosnia and Herzegovina in the Round of 32 · winners of Group D (6 points, GD +4 in the Group Stage). Qualified for the Round of 16, where they face Belgium.

AUS.text:
## Reading
Australia bid farewell to the World Cup in the Round of 32, but by the finest of margins. Runners-up in Group D, they held Egypt to a 1-1 and forced a penalty shootout, where they were ultimately beaten. They left the field without being defeated in regulation.
## Performance
In the Group Stage, a balanced campaign: 2-0 over Turkey, a 2-0 loss to the United States and a 0-0 with Paraguay — four points and second place. In the Round of 32, they fell behind to Egypt (Emam Ashour, 13'), but equalized through a Mohamed Hany own goal at 55. In the shootout, only Irvine and Mabil converted, and the 4-2 sent the Australians home.
## By the numbers
Four matches, one win, two draws and one loss in regulation; 5 goals scored and 4 conceded. Runners-up in Group D, Australia are eliminated in the Round of 32, beaten only from the penalty spot.

PAR.text:
## Reading
Gustavo Alfaro's Paraguay's fairy-tale campaign ended in the Round of 16. After eliminating four-time champions Germany on penalties in the Round of 32, La Albirroja held France for more than an hour, but fell 1-0 to a Mbappé goal. They leave with their heads held high as one of the World Cup's great upsets.
## Performance
In the Group Stage they were 3rd in Group D: they conceded 4-1 to the United States, reacted by beating Turkey 1-0 (a lightning goal from Matías Galarza in the 2nd minute) and held a 0-0 with Australia, enough to advance among the best third-placed teams. In the Round of 32, they drew 1-1 with Germany and won on penalties 4-3, with goalkeeper Orlando Gill saving two kicks. In the Round of 16, they resisted France bravely until being overcome by Mbappé at 70'.
## By the numbers
Five matches: 1 win, 2 draws (one decided on penalties) and 2 losses. They finished 3rd in Group D (4 pts) and advanced among the best third-placed teams. In the Round of 32, they eliminated Germany on penalties (1-1, 4-3); in the Round of 16, 0-1 to France — eliminated. They end a historic campaign.

TUR.text:
## Reading
Vincenzo Montella's Turkey were one of the disappointments of the first round and are eliminated: they finished 4th in Group D, even with the win over the United States in the send-off. Despite names like Arda Güler and Hakan Çalhanoğlu, the Turks only got going when it was already too late.
## Performance
They lost their first two matches without scoring — 2-0 to Australia and 1-0 to Paraguay — and truly complicated their situation. In the final round, they finally woke up and beat the U.S. 3-2, with Arda Güler, Barış Alper Yılmaz and Kaan Ayhan (in stoppage time), but the result served only to avoid finishing bottom.
## By the numbers
GP3 · 1 win · 2 losses · 3 goals scored · 5 conceded (GD -2) · 4th in Group D with 3 points. Eliminated in the Group Stage.

EGY.text:
## Reading
Egypt are in the Round of 16 after a penalty shootout of pure composure. They got out of the group unbeaten and, in the Round of 32, needed the spot kicks to overcome a battling Australia — but they marched on with authority from the mark.
## Performance
In Group G they earned five points without a loss: 1-1 with Belgium, 3-1 over New Zealand and 1-1 with Iran. In the Round of 32, Emam Ashour opened the scoring at 13, but an own goal leveled everything and took the game to penalties. There, Egypt were perfect with their first kicks — Saber, Rabia, Salah and Abdelmaguid — and closed it out 4-2.
## By the numbers
Four matches unbeaten in regulation, with 6 goals scored and 4 conceded. Runners-up in Group G, Egypt face Argentina in the Round of 16 on July 7.

NZL.text:
## Reading
New Zealand bid farewell to the World Cup. They finished last in Group G, without wins and with a defense that conceded ten goals in three matches.
## Performance
They lost 3-1 to Egypt in the opener (Finn Surman scored, but conceded the comeback), grabbed a surprising 2-2 with Iran — Elijah Just shone with two goals — and were overrun 5-1 by Belgium in the send-off. The point against Iran was the only one of the campaign.
## By the numbers
GP3 · 1D 2L · 4 goals scored, 10 conceded, GD -6 and only 1 point. Fourth and last in Group G — eliminated. Elijah Just, with three goals, was the team's attacking figure.

CIV.text:
## Reading
Emerse Faé's Ivory Coast made history in the Group Stage and reached the knockouts, but the run stopped in the Round of 32: they lost to Norway 2-1 and were eliminated, despite battling to the end.
## Performance
They qualified 2nd in Group E with 6 points: 1-0 over Ecuador (Amad Diallo at 90'), a 2-1 loss to Germany and 2-0 over Curaçao (two for Nicolas Pépé). A competitive side that won the head-to-head clashes for the spot.
## Round of 32
Against Norway, the Elephants fell behind to Antonio Nusa's goal (39'), equalized through Amad Diallo (74'), but conceded to Erling Haaland at 86' and lost 2-1.
## By the numbers
GP4 · 2-1 loss to Norway in the Round of 32 · eliminated (2nd in Group E, 6 points). The end of a historic campaign.

ECU.text:
## Reading
Sebastián Beccacece's Ecuador's run came to an end in the Round of 32. Third in Group E and qualified among the best third-placed teams, Ecuador were overcome by host nation Mexico 2-0 and bid farewell to the World Cup.
## Performance
In the Group Stage they earned 4 points — one win, one draw and one loss — with a disciplined defense (only 2 goals conceded), but little finishing (2 scored). In the Round of 32, they ran into an efficient Mexico: they conceded both goals in the first half (Quiñones and Jiménez) and could not react, finishing with ten after Piero Hincapié's sending-off at 90+5'.
## By the numbers
Group Stage: GP3 · 1 win · 1 draw · 1 loss · 2 goals scored · 2 conceded (GD 0) · 3rd in Group E with 4 points (qualified as a best third-placed team). In the Round of 32, a 2-0 loss to Mexico and elimination. End of the road at the World Cup.

CUW.text:
## Reading
Curaçao, led by veteran Dick Advocaat, bid farewell to their first World Cup in the Group Stage, but left with their heads held high: one of the smallest nations ever to play at a World Cup earned a historic point in Group E. They lacked the fighting spark against the stronger sides.
## Performance
They conceded 7-1 to Germany in the opener (goal from Livano Comenencia), but surprised by holding a goalless draw with Ecuador, earning their first-ever point at a World Cup. In the send-off, they lost 2-0 to Ivory Coast. Defensively fragile in the bigger games, but battling in the direct duel.
## By the numbers
GP3 · 1 draw · 2 losses · 1 goal scored · 9 conceded (GD -8) · 4th in Group E with 1 point. Eliminated in the Group Stage, but with a historic point on their World Cup debut.

KOR.text:
## Reading
Hong Myung-bo's South Korea are eliminated. They finished the Group Stage 3rd in Group A with 3 points and, among the twelve third-placed teams, came in 10th — outside the cut of the eight that advanced. Mexico (9 pts) dominated the group and South Africa (4 pts) took the other spot; Son Heung-min's side lacked attacking sharpness in the decisive matches.
## Performance
They debuted with a comeback win over the Czech Republic 2-1: Ladislav Krejci opened for the Czechs (59'), but Hwang In-beom (67') and Oh Hyeon-gyu (80') turned it around. They dropped off afterward — they lost to leaders Mexico 1-0 (Luis Romo, 50') and, in the final round, were overcome by South Africa 1-0 (Thapelo Maseko, 63'), a result that left them behind in Group A and, in the end, out of the knockouts.
## By the numbers
GP3 · 1W 2L · 2 goals scored, 3 conceded, GD -1 and 3 points. Third in Group A and 10th among the third-placed teams — eliminated. Hwang In-beom and Oh Hyeon-gyu scored the South Korean goals.

CZE.text:
## Reading
Miroslav Koubek's Czech Republic had a campaign to forget and finished bottom of Group A, eliminated. They earned just 1 point in three matches and suffered too much at the back, especially against hosts Mexico in the send-off. Even with Patrik Schick and Tomáš Souček, they lacked the solidity to sustain results.
## Performance
They lost to South Korea 2-1 in the opener (goal from Ladislav Krejčí), drew with South Africa 1-1 (Michal Sadílek) and were thrashed 3-0 by Mexico in the final round. The attack produced in the first games, but the defense collapsed and the team bids farewell without a win.
## By the numbers
GP3 · 1 draw · 2 losses · 2 goals scored · 6 conceded (GD -4) · bottom of Group A with 1 point. Eliminated in the Group Stage.

RSA.text:
## Reading
Hugo Broos's South Africa had a brave campaign, but stopped in the Round of 32. After surprising by qualifying 2nd in Group A, Bafana Bafana held Canada for almost the entire game and only conceded in stoppage time, eliminated 1-0. They left with their heads held high.
## Performance
In the Group Stage they lost to Mexico (0-2), drew with the Czech Republic (1-1) and won the direct clash with South Korea 1-0 (Maseko, 63'), securing the spot. In the Round of 32, they defended well against Canada, but conceded Eustáquio's goal at 90+2' and were eliminated.
## By the numbers
Group Stage: 1W 1D 1L, 2 goals scored, 3 conceded (GD -1), 2nd in Group A (4 pts). In the Round of 32, eliminated by Canada 1-0. They ended the World Cup among the top 32.

ALG.text:
## Reading
Algeria's campaign ended in the Round of 32. Qualified as one of the best third-placed teams from Group J, they ran into a more efficient Switzerland and fell 2-0, unable to mount the reaction they needed.
## Performance
In the group, Algeria fluctuated: they lost 3-0 to Argentina, reacted with a 2-1 over Jordan and grabbed a dramatic 3-3 with Austria that secured the spot on points. In the Round of 32, however, they saw Switzerland go up 2-0 early in the second half and had no answer — only a yellow card for Chaïbi and another for Boudaoui to show for it.
## By the numbers
Four matches, one win, two draws and one loss; 5 goals scored and 9 conceded. Third in Group J, Algeria are eliminated in the Round of 32.

JOR.text:
## Reading
World Cup debutants, Jamal Sellami's Jordan bid farewell in the Group Stage, without a point, but not without leaving their mark. The Jordanians found the net in all three Group J matches — a notable feat for a debutant — even though defensive fragility cost them dearly. They finished last, eliminated, but take home the historic record of the country's first goal at a World Cup.
## Performance
They lost to Austria 3-1 in the opener, with Ali Olwan scoring Jordan's first-ever World Cup goal. In the following round, against Algeria — their big chance — they even led through Nizar Al-Rashdan in the 36th minute, but conceded the comeback 2-1 (Nadhir Benbouali and Amine Gouiri) and were left with no hope of qualifying. In the finale, they still troubled Argentina with a goal from Mousa Al-Tamari, but were beaten 3-1.
## By the numbers
GP3 · 3 losses · 3 goals scored · 8 conceded (GD -5) · 4th and last in Group J, eliminated. Goals from Ali Olwan, Nizar Al-Rashdan and Mousa Al-Tamari (one each). End of the road: Jordan bid farewell to their first World Cup with the defense as their weak point, but with the certainty that they know how to score at this level.

SCO.text:
## Reading
Steve Clarke's Scotland are eliminated. They finished the Group Stage 3rd in Group C with 3 points and, among the twelve third-placed teams, came in 11th — outside the cut of the eight that advanced. Brazil (7 pts) and Morocco (7 pts) took the direct spots; the Scots were left with the feeling of a missed opportunity after a promising opener.
## Performance
They started well, beating Haiti 1-0 with a goal from John McGinn (28'), but dropped off in output. They lost to Morocco 1-0 to a lightning goal from Ismael Saibari early in the 2nd minute and were overrun by Brazil 3-0 — two from Vinícius Júnior (7' and 45+3') and one from Matheus Cunha (60') — without mounting a reaction. They lacked attacking punch: only one goal in three matches.
## By the numbers
GP3 · 1W 2L · 1 goal scored, 4 conceded, GD -3 and 3 points. Third in Group C and 11th among the third-placed teams — eliminated. McGinn scored the campaign's only goal.

MAR.text:
## Reading
Walid Regragui's Morocco are in the Quarterfinals. After eliminating the Netherlands on penalties in the Round of 32, the Atlas Lions overran host nation Canada 3-0 in the Round of 16, with Azzedine Ounahi in inspired form. Solid at the back and efficient up front, Morocco march on and dream big, repeating their standout run from 2022.
## Performance
In the Group Stage, they held Brazil to a 1-1 (Saibari opened, Vinícius Júnior equalized), beat Scotland 1-0 (Saibari in the 2nd minute) and closed out with 4-2 over Haiti. In the Round of 32, they drew 1-1 with the Netherlands and advanced on penalties (3-2). In the Round of 16, they dominated Canada: Ounahi scored at 50 and at 82, and Rahimi made it three at 90+8, in the 3-0 that stamped a spot among the last eight.
## By the numbers
Five matches, unbeaten in regulation: 3 wins and 2 draws (one of them decided on penalties). In the Group Stage, 2nd in Group C (7 pts, same as Brazil, behind only on goal difference). In the Round of 32, 1-1 with the Netherlands and qualification on penalties; in the Round of 16, 3-0 over Canada. Top scorer: Ismael Saibari (3 goals). Next challenge: the winner of Paraguay vs. France, in the Quarterfinals.

HAI.text:
## Reading
Sébastien Migné's Haiti bid farewell to the World Cup in the Group Stage, eliminated with three losses in Group C. Despite the effort, they lacked defensive consistency: the Caribbean side conceded goals in every match and finished bottom, without points.
## Performance
They lost to Scotland 1-0 in the opener, were overcome by Brazil 3-0 (Matheus Cunha twice and Vinícius Júnior) and fell 4-2 to Morocco, when at least they found the net through Wilson Isidor and a Bounou own goal. Competitive in some passages, but fragile in the duels with the stronger sides.
## By the numbers
GP3 · 3 losses · 2 goals scored · 8 conceded (GD -6) · bottom of Group C, without points. Eliminated in the Group Stage.

KSA.text:
## Reading
Saudi Arabia bid farewell to the World Cup. They finished last in Group H, without wins, and paid dearly for the rout suffered in the opener. The two draws that followed were not enough to dream of qualifying.
## Performance
They conceded 4-0 to Spain in the opener, a result that weighed on the goal difference. They reacted with a 1-1 against Uruguay, with Abdulelah Alamri (41') scoring, and held a 0-0 with Cape Verde in the final round — but they lacked firepower to advance.
## By the numbers
GP3 · 2D 1L · 1 goal scored, 5 conceded, GD -4 and 2 points. Fourth and last in Group H — eliminated.

ESP.text:
## Reading
Spain stayed flawless: they thrashed Austria 3-0 in the Round of 32 and advanced to the Round of 16 without any scares, keeping their defense impenetrable. With Mikel Oyarzabal in great form, La Roja establish themselves as title contenders.
## Performance
They dominated Group H in first place, with the best defense in the section and without conceding a goal: 0-0 with Cape Verde, 4-0 over Saudi Arabia (goals from Yamal and Oyarzabal, among others) and 1-0 over Uruguay, through Álex Baena.
## Round of 32
Against Austria, Oyarzabal opened the scoring (36'), Pedro Porro doubled it (66') and Oyarzabal closed the account (89'), sealing the 3-0 with authority — another game without conceding.
## By the numbers
GP4 · 3-0 win over Austria in the Round of 32 · winners of Group H (7 points, without conceding a goal in the Group Stage, GD +5). Standout: Mikel Oyarzabal, with two goals. Qualified for the Round of 16, where they face Portugal.

CPV.text:
## Reading
Cape Verde bid farewell to the World Cup with their heads held high. Debutants and underdogs, the island had a historic campaign: they got out of the group unbeaten and were only eliminated by Argentina in extra time, after scaring the favorites to the end.
## Performance
In the Group Stage, three draws worth their weight in gold: 0-0 with Spain, 2-2 with Uruguay and 0-0 with Saudi Arabia — three points and an unprecedented qualification in second in Group H. In the Round of 32, they sold themselves dearly: they equalized twice against Argentina, through Deroy Duarte (59') and Sidny Lopes Cabral (103'), and only conceded in the 3-2 extra time.
## By the numbers
Four matches without winning in regulation, but only one loss — in extra time; 4 goals scored and 5 conceded. Runners-up in Group H, Cape Verde are eliminated in the Round of 32, ending a memorable debut.

URU.text:
## Reading
Uruguay were one of the biggest disappointments of the first round. Without a win in Group H — two draws and one loss — La Celeste finished third with just 2 points and are eliminated: among the twelve third-placed teams they came in last, well short of the cut of the eight that advanced. Spain (7 pts) dominated the group and Cape Verde (3 pts), on their World Cup debut, took the second spot.
## Performance
They debuted by conceding a 2-2 to Cape Verde: they fell behind to Kevin Pina (21'), turned it around still in the first half through Maxi Araujo (44') and Canobbio (45+6'), but were caught again by Hélio Varela (61'). Against Saudi Arabia they were held to a 1-1 — Abdulelah Alamri opened for the Saudis (41') and Maxi Araujo leveled everything (80'). In the send-off, they lost 1-0 to Spain, goal from Álex Baena (42'), and bid farewell to the World Cup without a single win.
## By the numbers
GP3 · 2D 1L · 3 goals scored, 4 conceded, GD -1 and just 2 points. Third in Group H and 12th (last) among the third-placed teams — eliminated. Maxi Araujo, with 2 goals, was the only attacking bright spot of a campaign to forget.

IRN.text:
## Reading
Iran are eliminated by the finest of margins. Unbeaten in the Group Stage — three draws — they finished 3rd in Group G and, among the twelve third-placed teams, came in 9th, one place below the cut: the eighth and last to qualify, Senegal, took the spot on goal difference (+2 against Iran's zero). They lacked the ability to turn solidity into wins.
## Performance
They conceded a 2-2 to New Zealand chasing the game twice: Elijah Just opened (7' and 54') for the Kiwis and Ramin Rezaeian (32') and Mohammad Mohebbi (64') responded. They held a solid 0-0 against Belgium — even with a man advantage after Nathan Ngoy's sending-off (66') — and closed with a 1-1 against Egypt: Mahmoud Saber opened (5') and Rezaeian leveled soon after (14'). No losses, but no wins.
## By the numbers
GP3 · 3D · 3 goals scored, 3 conceded, GD zero and 3 points. Third in Group G and 9th among the third-placed teams — eliminated on goal difference. Ramin Rezaeian, with 2 goals, was the attacking standout.

GER.text:
## Reading
A melancholy and early elimination. Julian Nagelsmann's Germany topped Group E, but fell right away in the Round of 32, surprised by Paraguay on penalties. Despite the devastating attack in the Group Stage, the four-time champions did not get past their knockout opener — the biggest upset of the World Cup so far.
## Performance
In the Group Stage, they debuted with a 7-1 rout of Curaçao, beat Ivory Coast 2-1 (two for Deniz Undav) and lost 2-1 to Ecuador when already qualified. In the Round of 32, they drew 1-1 with Paraguay — Kai Havertz leveled at 54' after Enciso's goal — took the game to extra time and succumbed on penalties 4-3, with goalkeeper Gill saving the kicks of Havertz and Woltemade.
## By the numbers
Group Stage: 2W 1L, 10 goals scored, 4 conceded (GD +6), 1st in Group E (6 pts). In the Round of 32, eliminated by Paraguay (1-1, 3-4 on penalties). Top scorer in the first round: Deniz Undav (3 goals). End of the road at the World Cup.

BEL.text:
## Reading
Belgium suffered like never before, but advanced in epic fashion: they went down 2-0 to Senegal, found the equalizer late and won 3-2 in extra time, with Youri Tielemans deciding it in stoppage time of the extra period. A spot in the Round of 16 with plenty of drama.
## Performance
They advanced in first in Group G, but gave a scare: two draws (1-1 with Egypt, 0-0 with Iran) before thrashing New Zealand 5-1, securing first place on goal difference ahead of Egypt.
## Round of 32
Senegal went up 2-0 through Habib Diarra (24') and Ismaila Sarr (51'). But the Red Devils reacted: Romelu Lukaku pulled one back (86') and Youri Tielemans equalized (89'), taking the game to extra time. Already at 120+5', Tielemans scored again and completed the 3-2 comeback.
## By the numbers
GP4 · 3-2 comeback win over Senegal in extra time of the Round of 32 · winners of Group G (5 points, GD +4 in the Group Stage). Knockout hero: Youri Tielemans. Qualified for the Round of 16, where they face the United States.

AUT.text:
## Reading
Ralf Rangnick's Austria reached the Round of 32 buoyed by the coach's organization, but ran into Spain: they lost 3-0 and bid farewell to the World Cup, with no answer for La Roja.
## Performance
They qualified 2nd in Group J with 4 points: a 3-1 win over Jordan, a 2-0 loss to Argentina and a dramatic 3-3 draw with Algeria (Kalajdzic saving it at 90+6').
## Round of 32
Against Spain, Austria resisted for more than half an hour, but gave way: Mikel Oyarzabal opened the scoring (36'), Pedro Porro doubled it (66') and Oyarzabal closed (89') the 3-0 that eliminated the Austrians.
## By the numbers
GP4 · 3-0 loss to Spain in the Round of 32 · eliminated. They had qualified 2nd in Group J (4 points). End of the campaign.

BRA.text:
## Reading
Carlo Ancelotti's Brazil are in the Round of 16. Winners of Group C, they came from behind to beat Japan 2-1 in the Round of 32 and remain firmly in the title race, backed by the best defense of the first round and an attack led by Vinícius Jr and Matheus Cunha. But they had to sweat: the Seleção fell behind before experience spoke louder in the second half.
## Performance
In the Group Stage they got going after the 1-1 with Morocco, with 3-0 wins over Haiti and Scotland. In the Round of 32 they got a scare: Kaishu Sano opened for Japan (29'), but Casemiro equalized in the second half (56') and Gabriel Martinelli sealed the comeback in stoppage time (90+5'). Vinícius Jr (4 goals in the Group Stage) and Cunha (3) remain the attacking references.
## By the numbers
Group Stage: 2W 1D, 7 goals scored, 1 conceded, 2 clean sheets, 1st in Group C (7 pts, GD +6). In the Round of 32, they beat Japan 2-1. Now they await in the Round of 16 the winner of Ivory Coast vs. Norway.

MEX.text:
## Reading
The host nation march on flawlessly at the World Cup. Javier Aguirre's Mexico won Group A with a 100% record and without conceding a goal and, in the Round of 32, beat Ecuador 2-0 to stamp a spot in the Round of 16 — four wins, eight goals scored and none conceded at the World Cup.
## Performance
In the Group Stage, they overran opponents: 3-0 over the Czech Republic, 1-0 over South Korea and 2-0 over South Africa, clear leaders and an impenetrable defense. In the Round of 32, in front of their fans, they settled it early with Julián Quiñones (22') and Raúl Jiménez (31') and controlled the game to the end — they even saw Ecuador finish with ten after Piero Hincapié's sending-off.
## By the numbers
Group Stage: GP3 · 3 wins · 6 goals scored · 0 conceded (GD +6) · 1st in Group A with 9 points. In the Round of 32, 2-0 over Ecuador (Quiñones and Jiménez). In total: 4 matches, 8 goals scored, none conceded. Next challenge: the Round of 16.

ARG.text:
## Reading
Argentina remain alive and in the Round of 16, but they had to sweat to get there. After a flawless Group Stage, they suffered more than expected against debutants Cape Verde and only advanced in extra time.
## Performance
In Group J they went three wins from three: 3-0 over Algeria, 2-0 over Austria and 3-1 over Jordan — nine points and a comfortable lead. In the Round of 32, the script got complicated: Messi opened the scoring at 29, but Cape Verde equalized twice (Deroy Duarte and Sidny Lopes Cabral) and took the game to the extra period. Lisandro Martínez scored at 92 and a Diney Borges own goal, at 111, sealed the 3-2.
## By the numbers
Four matches, four wins, 11 goals scored and only 3 conceded — a 100% campaign. Winners of Group J, Argentina face Egypt in the Round of 16 on July 7.
